item.title: Oklahoma Principal, Kirk Moore, getting crowned Prom King by the students he saved two weeks after taking down a would-be school shooter.
item.dc:creator: kenistod

item.title: My friend is a libertarian. He works for the government.
item.dc:creator: kenistod

item.title: So you're considering Linux
item.description:
Hi. If you follow any tech news it's probably popped up on your radar that a lot of people are switching to Linux to get away from Microslop's antics. If you're considering the switch but got overwhelmed by the sheer amount of information and tips on the subject, I'm here to make it simple for you. First of all: if you rely on Windows-only software for your paycheck: don't make the switch or be ready to do dual-booting. Most notably if you need Adobe software. Yes, it can technically be installed but might not be stable. You could use virtual machines but many of those don't provide GPU acceleration (necessary for Adobe stuff) and even when they do, and you get your Photoshop running well, it just adds points of failure. An update to either the VM or your OS might break compatibility at some point. Also: if you aren't 100% sure about the Linux switch, back up your Windows partition using this: https://rescuezilla.com/ That way you can just go back to whatever you had before you started your Linux experiment. Other than that, here we go: so you've read people praise Linux over Windows or iOS, because it's free and open source, free of big tech, free of spying and adware. You've heard that you have direct control over what gets installed on your computer and if and when updates are installed.
You googled "Linux" and immediately got a headache over how many options there are and how much knowledge there is to learn on the subject. Unlike Windows, there isn't a single, unified "Linux" as such. What does that mean, exactly? The underpinning of the OS is the Linux kernel. The thing that makes it work at all. But to work with you, a human, rather than just the computer, there are layers on top of which you have a choice. Most importantly: the desktop environment. To simplify: the desktop environment is how your OS will look and feel to you. It's the interface. There is a large variety of environments out there but the most popular options are: KDE Plasma, Gnome, XFCE, Cinnamon and Budgie. So a Linux distribution - the thing you actually install as an OS has the Linux kernel, a desktop environment chosen by the developers and a selection of basic software to go along with it so that you don't have to install stuff like a calculator or notepad yourself. Maybe a few modifications to those as well. Here's how each of those looks. KDE Plasma:
Gnome:
XFCE:
Cinnamon:
Budgie:
Like I said: I will keep it simple: go with distros that have KDE Plasma options. It's very beginner-friendly and supports stuff like variable refresh rate and HDR. Some of the other ones do too, some are just starting to implement it, some don't for now. A lot of people will recommend Gnome as well but if you're coming from Windows, it will feel rather alien to you. KDE feels more or less like Windows does and offers good performance. Now, the all important question: which Linux distribution should you choose? Unlike those "top 10" lists I will give you 3 options, choose according to your use case: PikaOS: https://wiki.pika-os.com/en/home
It's based on Ubuntu (like MANY others), which means there are faster alternatives out there in terms of raw performance but this particular one is a bit faster as it uses kernel optimizations to get better FPS in games, in particular. It has a software store so you won't ever really have to open the terminal or google stuff in order to find and install software. But it may not be the newest available version by default. It's very beginner-friendly and has a Gnome, KDE Plasma, Hyprland, Niri and Cosmic editions (like I said: just go with KDE). CachyOS: https://cachyos.org/
If you've ended up on https://distrowatch.com/ you know it as the number 1 distro at the moment and it's what I currently use. If you're after FPS in games and such, it's a fantastic choice. It comes with a huge variety of desktop environments which you can choose while installing. BUT beware: it's not very beginner-friendly. Doesn't really have a software store. It does have a repository GUI (so only a small selection of software is findable in there), but a lot of the time you WILL need to use terminal utilities such as Paru, Yay, Snap, Flatpak etc. to install and update software. If that sounds like black magic to you, don't go that route. Finally: antiX: https://antixlinux.com/
This option is for those of you who found a 20+ years old computer in your attic and want it to have some use, still. AntiX will run very well on absolute potatoes. There are other Linux distros that focus on this as well, but I've found this one to be the best because unlike Puppy Linux, for one, it's a fully-fledged Linux that will allow you to install any software without excessive tinkering. BUT: the interface is very nerdy and might feel overly complicated to newcomers. Also, language packs might be a big hit and miss on this one as I installed Polish on this thing and some interface items were translated while others remained in English. It's not the most polished (no pun intended) distro out there but if you want to revive that old PC, this is a fantastic choice nonetheless. Ok, so now that we know which desktop environment to pick and which distro to pick, what about games and software. Good news for you, gamers! Valve has developed a compatibility layer called Proton which allows almost all Windows games to run on Linux. That means the Windows games you have on Steam will attempt to run through it and if you have a standalone (non-steam) games, you can add them and run through Proton as well. On top of that, there are fantastic Linux utilities like the Heroic Game Launcher and Lutris that will run the games you've purchased through other storefronts like GOG, for instance, through Proton or Wine (similar thing to Proton) as well. One more BUT: esports titles that require OS-level anticheat software are no-go at this point, because the developers of that software could give a damn. This is subject to change in the future, though.
As for software, the Linux community have made great strides over the last few years. There are great alternatives to Windows software, often better than their paid counterparts: Microslop Office - Only Office, LibreOffice Email clients: Mozilla Thunderbird, KMail Adobe Premiere - DaVinci Resolve Adobe Photoshop, Lightroom - Photopea (browser-based), Krita, Gimp, Darktable Audio editors: Audacity FL Studio: Bitwig, Reaper, Ardour Media players - VLC Torrent clients: qBittorrent And those are just a few examples. There is always an alternative. And the majority will be free, open source, won't have spyware, adware and subscriptions, just like Linux itself. Now that you've made the decision, picked your distro and found the Linux alternatives for the software you need, how do you install Linux? Well, not all distros use the same installer but they are all similar and easy to follow, for the most part. The actual important part is the partitioning of your drive. If you only want Linux, just pick the "erase disk and install" option. If you're doing a dual-boot with Windows, go with the default option or do manual partitioning (install Windows first to make this thing a whole lot easier, if you're doing a clean install of both). This is the part that requires you to know what you're doing. If you don't, give your nerdy friend a beer and they'll do it for you.
If you want to learn more about Linux I recommend watching some related YouTube channels. I watched The Linux Experiment for a year before I made the switch. I was also hell-bent on Fedora with Gnome at that point, but upon installing it, I realized it's not for me, which brings me to my final point: Don't be afraid to do some distro-hopping and experimentation! if you don't want to commit to installing those on your actual computer, use VM software or try the Live versions (keep in mind those will be slower than an actual installed OS) I've given you 3 distro options to pick from but that doesn't mean the others are bad. That's the thing about Linux: the huge variety of options can be daunting but it also means you get to pick a distribution to fit your exact preferences and needs. Also, remember when computing was fun? When you got your first PC with, like, Windows 95 installed and you had no idea what you were doing but just experimenting with settings and software and games was just pure magic? That's what Linux is like. It's not Windows. There's stuff to learn, things to experiment with, problems to solve. By going Linux not only do you free yourself of all the BS Microslop is trying to force on you, but you're also making your computer truly YOURS again and bringing back the fun. Let that sink in. Cheers.

item.dc:creator: LogicDude

item.title: Saving Private Dump
item.description:
My favorite card game as a kid was pretending I was Gambit and throwing cards across the room
Comics can be anything you want them to be
Doc Savage and supporting goons
I doubt they are all sitting around singing “he’s got the whole world in his hands”
“A real hero. Like a man, but better. Swell, wouldn’t that be Super! I know, he’s a Super Man! Superman!” “An Ubermensch?” “Put a sock in it, they aren’t ready for that yet, it’s only the 30s!” -Siegel and Shuster probably
“Ok Bob, there’s all my ideas. What do you think?” “Shut the door on your way out.” -Finger and Kane probably
Sigh
Ah, I see, that’s what I’ve been doing
Slay
Slay
Conflict is their commodity, defend as peacefully as we can
I’d like to be this dapper
Good lord that’s horrifying, wanna go look for it?
Yaaaar… not that I even want the garbage they’re selling. Enshittification is part of the program, if you can’t even imagine a better world, you won’t know what you’re missing. The quality of our art is reducing the quality of our suffering or something like that
Legislate in your fursuit, i don’t care
What did you do Jared?
Don’t see enough representation for the Pacific islands
Guys, the world is broke, I have no idea how this happened
Throw me in a tree! Let the Vultures have me! Record the sound of my bones falling and sample it to a funky beat!
They both look happier
Oh Flash! Thanks for putting the world back, I’ve been meaning to do that. Missed Bolivia, brb
Everything can be sold to you, aesthetics, ideology, sweet giant white socks
I’ve never made a choice in my life, because in my search for free will I’ve found a chasm of desire for every choice simultaneously… Wait… I see something with bbq sauce and I’m feeling a little yeehaw. Ooo crunchy onions!
Supposedly this is what inspired Percy to write Ozymandias
Usagi!
Why I don’t have roommates
Except my buried Terracotta Army under my bed, gotta have those guys just in case
Please brain
Trying to tell myself this
I hate that American Psycho is the one I’ve watched most, but I’m trying to understand what ails us
Keep as many weirdos around you as possible. It’s safer in packs
Kitties sleep in cuddle piles. Lemme get the goop out of her eye, poor baby Have a good day fellow earthlings and visitors!

item.title: Only Union Bears Allowed on the Poles ...
item.description: An Arizona lineman successfully coaxed a bear off a utility pole after it climbed to the top and was in danger of being electrocuted. The bear eventually got down from the pole and ran away unharmed. https://www.instagram.com/reel/DRuwQxkjq5X/

26 Imgur Creator Spotlight: @thecommoncolor painted this little guy. Fantastic work. Check out their other posts for more! Nominated by: @scotswahey
27 Man Tax: Manuel Kornisiuk
“Reflect upon your present blessings — of which every man has many — not on your past misfortunes, of which all men have some.” - Charles Dickens So, I made a discord server. It's a relaxed environment where you can take/donate memes to the cause, play trivia twice a week, chat about lots of topics or about nothing at all. We also have Jack Box game night and are setting up movie night! Very laid back/relaxed vibe. Come hang with us. https://discord.gg/AWFV7FT3RQ I'm also on the imgcat server for updates & fun: https://discord.gg/TpjD37jgJ9 https://ko-fi.com/mememuseumcurator Unable to work, zero income. Feel free to leave me a tip. Those that have, y'all are the bee's knees. You've honestly been my lifeline. (Apparently doesn't work in firefox). For the couple people that have said I'm "scamming" people, I only get SNAP, so this money goes to things like TP, shampoo, laundry detergent, etc. ***Tag me if you see an artist/creator you like whose work isn't getting seen as much as it should be! Or if you are an artist/creator and want me to post something of yours, DM me
item.dc:creator: MemeMuseumCurator

item.title: No electricity. No problem. A Redditor sent us a laptop to our project in Yemen
item.description: Huge shoutout to the legend who sent us this laptop ? He’s a software engineer and included a ton of coding material that we’ll add to our server for others to learn from. Whish i could tag, but his account us deleted. With summer coming, power cuts here in Yemen are getting longer and more frequent—sometimes most of the day. A laptop like this, that can run during blackouts, makes a huge difference for us. Thank you again for the support over the years. It genuinely means everything and is changing lives here ❤️ You can support us here https://www.gofundme.com/f/help-us-keep-an-internet-cafe-in-yemen-running?modal=donations&tab=all
item.dc:creator: maho90

item.dc:creator: wolfwinter

item.title: Potato's Daily Dirties
item.description:
The above image was made by the wonderful CammyJay! They have their own channel in the deviousness discord where they share artwork and sometimes do drawings based on word prompts given by members. its a lot of fun! I has a Deviousness discord server! We do have a hard rule of no politics and/or religion. The discord is a safe space to get away from the bullshit going on. We have a bunch of areas including a SFW area, content creator area, a NSFW area and more! We have gaming fun! We have spontaneous gaming and some ping roles for people to use to get groups together based on US and EU time zones! We have movie nights on Saturdays! So feel free to wander in and say haii! Thank you to everyone who has joined and made it such a unique and special place! Deviousness discord that is 18+: https://discord.gg/deviousness If you have issues with link see dis: https://imgur.com/a/8GUNAz4 Deviousness steam group: https://steamcommunity.com/groups/DeviousnessDiscord
artist of work above (aside from my signing potato on it): https://florahv.artstation.com/projects/XnL8P3 have been traveling a lot. so if anyone has united milageplus things they wont use like miles/pluspoints they wouldnt mind gifting or using for my future travel things lemme know via dm here. I am on dis now and trying to remember to linky over there too for when I post and also post the memes that get removed: https://bsky.app/profile/potatocheesmonk.bsky.social I has a post with links to all my past posts. enjoy: https://imgur.com/a/ZDA2CBe #potato #memes #dailydirties #makinmywaydowntown #holdinhandstothefrontpage
item.dc:creator: PotatoCheesMonk

item.title: Lyft driver kicks racist couple out of his car.

item.title: Crown shyness is a phenomenon where the top branches of neighboring trees avoid touching to stay safe, leaving visible jigsaw like gaps between their crowns.
item.dc:creator: bohund
